title: Container Threat Models, Users, Capabilities, Seccomp, Rootless, and Daemon Authority · Docker Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares fresh container threat models, users, capabilities, seccomp, rootless, and daemon authority decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.
Run as a deliberate non-root UID/GID and reconcile image ownership, mounted files, rootless mappings, and required privileged startup work.
Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel · changed case · Quiz Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz document parser runs privileged as root, mounts daemon authority, writes the image filesystem, has network access, and no resource caps. Remove authority and state the remaining shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.

Which action demonstrates Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel · changed case · Quiz Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default. For Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel · changed case · Quiz Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel, keep Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.
